title: salix glauca subsp. stipulifera
intro: Salix glauca subsp. stipulifera, also known as Siberian willow, is a subspecies of willow tree native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. The medicinal properties of Salix glauca subsp. stipulifera include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Salix glauca subsp. stipulifera include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
